heading: Director of the Government Publishing Office: appointment
label: 301 Director of the Government Publishing Office: appointment
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on 44 U.S. Code, 1964 ed., § 31 (, ; , ; , ; , ; , ).Jan. 12, 1895, ch. 23, § 1728 Stat. 603June 12, 1917, ch. 27, § 140 Stat. 173Feb. 20, 1923, ch. 9842 Stat. 1278Mar. 4, 1925, ch. 549, § 143 Stat. 1299May 29, 1928, ch. 90945 Stat. 1006Changes are made in phraseology.

Amendments2022— designated existing provisions as subsec. (a) and added subsecs. (b) and (c).Pub. L. 117–2132014—, struck out “, who must be a practical printer and versed in the art of bookbinding,” after “suitable person” and substituted “The” for “His”.Pub. L. 113–235, § 1301(g), substituted “Director of the Government Publishing Office” for “Public Printer” in section catchline and text.Pub. L. 113–235, § 1301(c)1972— struck out “; bond” in section catchline, and provisions from text which required the Public Printer to give a bond in the sum of $25,000.Pub. L. 92–310

Change of Name, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–235, div. H, title I, § 1301(d)128 Stat. 2537Dec. 16, 2014“Any reference in any law other than in title 44, United States Code, or in any rule, regulation, certificate, directive, instruction, or other official paper in force on the date of enactment of this Act [] to the Public Printer shall be considered to refer and apply to the Director of the Government Publishing Office.”Dec. 16, 2014

Application of 2022 Amendment to Incumbent, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–213, § 1(b)136 Stat. 2251Oct. 17, 2022“If there is an individual serving in the position of Director of the Government Publishing Office, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, on the date of enactment of this Act []—Oct. 17, 2022“(1)the amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to the appointment of such individual; and“(2)the term of the individual for purposes of subsection (b) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall be considered to have started on the date on which the individual assumed the office of Director of the Government Publishing Office.”section 301 of title 44

children.label: a
children.text: The President of the United States shall nominate and, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, appoint a suitable person to take charge of and manage the Government Publishing Office. The title shall be Director of the Government Publishing Office.

children.label: b
children.text: The Director shall be appointed for a term of 10 years.

children.label: c
children.text: An individual appointed to the position of Director, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, may be reappointed to that position in accordance with subsections (a) and (b).
